chief_complaint: Recurrent wheezing and shortness of breath
transcript_text: Doctor: What brings you in today? Patient: I have had wheezing and shortness of breath for the past month, especially at night and early morning. It also gets worse with exercise. Doctor: How often do you wake up at night? Patient: About twice a week. Doctor: Any triggers you have noticed? Patient: Cold air and dust definitely make it worse. Doctor: Are you using any inhalers? Patient: I tried an OTC one but it only helps briefly.

chief_complaint: Chronic cough and progressive dyspnea on exertion
transcript_text: Doctor: What brings you in today? Patient: I have had a chronic cough for years and lately I get short of breath just walking up one flight of stairs. Doctor: Do you smoke? Patient: I smoked a pack a day for 20 years, quit five years ago. Doctor: Any sputum production? Patient: Yes, mostly in the mornings, whitish. Doctor: Any swelling in your legs? Patient: A little bit around the ankles at the end of the day.

chief_complaint: Productive cough with fever for 5 days
transcript_text: Doctor: Tell me about your symptoms. Patient: I have had a bad cough for five days, bringing up yellowish green phlegm. I had a fever of 101 degrees two days ago. Doctor: Any chest pain? Patient: Yes, on the right side when I take a deep breath. Doctor: Shortness of breath? Patient: Yes, even at rest today, which is new. Doctor: Any recent sick contacts? Patient: My grandson had a bad cold two weeks ago. Doctor: Any chills or night sweats? Patient: Yes, chills and I woke up sweating last night.

Doctor: How have you been managing your asthma since January? Patient: Not well. I have been using my rescue inhaler almost every day this week, sometimes twice a day. I also wake up coughing about three nights a week. Doctor: Are you using the Budesonide inhaler as prescribed? Patient: I try but I forget the morning dose sometimes. Doctor: Any new triggers? Patient: Cold air and exercise are still the worst. Yesterday I had to stop my walk halfway because I could not catch my breath. Doctor: Any wheezing? Patient: Yes, especially in the mornings. My peak flow has been about 60 to 70 percent.

chief_complaint: COPD acute exacerbation with increased dyspnea and purulent sputum
transcript_text: Doctor: What is different from your last visit? Patient: I have been much more short of breath for the past three days. Even walking from the bedroom to the bathroom leaves me gasping. Doctor: Any change in your sputum? Patient: Yes it turned yellow and there is much more of it. Doctor: Any fever? Patient: Low grade, about 99.5 yesterday. Doctor: Are you using your Tiotropium every day? Patient: Yes but it does not seem to be helping. I have been using my rescue inhaler every couple of hours. Doctor: What did your home oxygen monitor show? Patient: It showed 91 this morning which scared me.
